457 Madison Ave

From: John Amerling
[mailto:jamerling@rrcaribbean.com]
Sent: Friday, August 18, 2006 4:22 PM
To: Emad Hanna
Cc: Darren Indyke; Rich Kahn
Subject: FW: Windows

It might make sense to forward this to Mr. Epstein,
but that would be
your call.

From: John Amerling
[mailto:jamerling@rrcaribbean.com]
Sent: Friday, August 18, 2006 4:19 PM
To: 'dougdstine@aol.com'
Cc: 'Sean O'Connell'
Subject: RE: Windows

Thank you for the information regarding the window
quotations. I have
the following comments:

  1. We are obligated to compare quotations in our
    scoping
    activities; have competitive quotations been
    obtained here? The pricing
    here is at least 3 times the amounts which we were
    paying for the
    systems at the Ritz when we were building those
    units; we believe that a
    competitive solicitation should be conducted.

  2. While the glass may meet Dade county standards
    the Dade County
    approval process covers the windows as a system,
    frame, sash and
    installation; since the window is a custom one-off
    window it will never
    will be certified because custom work is too
    expensive to test and
    certify.

  3. Some other comments on issues which you may have
    covered with
    Mr. Epstein, if so ignore them:

    a. Out swinging casement windows are
    problematic here; they
    catch the wind which damages the hardware; moreover,
    if they open out
    over deck area outside they take up space; these
    windows also let in a
    lot of water in a sudden showers; awning windows, in
    contrast, provide
    air circulation and are not vulnerable to rain; I
    have some awning
    windows at my house which have not been closed for a
    year.
    b. We prefer PVC cladding here because aluminum
    finishes
    can become pitted over time; perhaps the quality of
    the finishes here
    can overcome this problem, but you can never
    underestimate the harshness
    of this environment right next to the sea.

There is no question that this company can do good
work. The entry to
your offices featured in their web site and the
other applications shown
there are impressive. We would not be doing our job,
however, if we did
not make this recommendation.

Please let me have any comments or questions which
you many have.

From: dougdstine@aol.com [mailto:dougdstine@aol.com]

Sent: Thursday, August 17, 2006 6:02 PM
To: jamerling@viaccess.net;
jamerling@rrcaribbean.com
Subject: Windows

John

Here is the information concerning the windows for
the office. Mr.
Molyneux would really like to use them. Please let
me know if you need
additional information. Thanks

Doug Stine
